267.reason: [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the token type is still set to AND, which is incorrect. This causes the lexer to output a token instead of only recording an error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the token type is still set to AND, which is incorrect. This causes the lexer to output a token instead of only recording an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the token type is still set to AND, which is incorrect. This causes the lexer to output a token instead of only reporting an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the token type is still set to AND, which causes invalid tokens to be added to the token list instead of halting or skipping properly. This leads to incorrect output in lexer.txt instead of error.txt.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the token type is still set to AND, which causes invalid tokens to be added to the token list instead of halting or skipping. This leads to incorrect output in lexer.txt even when an error exists.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '&' is encountered, an error is correctly added, but the token type is still set to AND, which is incorrect. It should not produce a valid token in error cases.]

276.reason: [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the token type is still set to OR, leading to incorrect token generation instead of pure error reporting.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the token type is still set to OR, leading to incorrect token generation instead of pure error reporting.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Similarly, when a single '|' is encountered, an error is reported, but the token type is still set to OR, leading to incorrect token generation instead of pure error handling.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: even when a single '|' is found and an error is recorded, the token type is still set to OR, causing an invalid token to be emitted instead of handling it as a pure lexical error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', an error is reported but the token type is still set to OR, causing an invalid token to be emitted instead of suppressing token generation upon lexical error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: for a single '|', an error is recorded but the token type is still set to OR, leading to invalid token output instead of only error reporting.]
